english: I am forced to speak of things which I would rather leave untouched...
What I am about to relate will seem incredible to those who have not witnessed such things...
Looking back, I realize that the warning signs were there from the beginning...
Had I known then what horrors awaited, I would never have...
It is with great reluctance that I set down these facts, knowing how they will be received...
The events I must now describe have left me a broken and haunted man...
polish: Jestem zmuszony mówić o rzeczach, które wolałbym pozostawić w cieniu...
To, co teraz opowiem, wyda się niewiarygodne tym, którzy nie byli świadkami takich rzeczy...
Patrząc wstecz, zdaję sobie sprawę, że znaki ostrzegawcze były widoczne od samego początku...
Gdybym wiedział wówczas, jakie koszmary na mnie czekały, nigdy bym nie...
Z wielką niechęcią spisuję te fakty, wiedząc jak zostaną przyjęte...
Wydarzenia, które muszę teraz opisać, uczyniły ze mnie złamanego i nawiedzanego człowieka...

english: No words in any human language can adequately convey...
What I witnessed defied all attempts at rational description...
The thing before me was something for which the term "horror" seems woefully inadequate...
I can find no words to express the nature of that which confronted me...
To call it merely frightening would be to miss the essential quality of cosmic wrongness...
Language itself seemed to crumble in the face of such alien unreality...
polish: Żadne słowa w żadnym ludzkim języku nie są w stanie odpowiednio oddać...
To, czego byłem świadkiem, wymykało się wszelkim próbom racjonalnego opisu...
Rzecz przede mną była czymś, dla czego słowo "groza" wydaje się żałośnie nieadekwatne...
Nie potrafię znaleźć słów, by wyrazić naturę tego, co mnie spotkało...
Nazwać to jedynie przerażającym, oznaczałoby przegapić istotną jakość kosmicznej nieprawości...
Sam język zdawał się kruszyć w obliczu tak obcej nierealności...

english: Now, after many years, I can finally bring myself to...
It has taken me decades to recover enough to set down these memories...
Only now, with the wisdom of hindsight, do I understand the true nature of...
The passage of time has done little to dim the horror of those events...
Even now, the mere recollection sends cold shivers through my frame...
polish: Teraz, po wielu latach, mogę wreszcie zdobyć się na...
Minęły dziesięciolecia, zanim doszedłem do siebie na tyle, by spisać te wspomnienia...
Dopiero teraz, z mądrością wstecznego spojrzenia, rozumiem prawdziwą naturę...
Upływ czasu niewiele uczynił, by złagodzić grozę tamtych wydarzeń...
Nawet teraz samo wspomnienie przeszywa moje ciało zimnym dreszczem...

english: At first, I noticed nothing amiss, but gradually a sense of wrongness crept over me...
The full horror of the situation dawned upon me by slow, terrible degrees...
Piece by piece, the dreadful truth assembled itself in my mind...
What had seemed merely odd now revealed its true and terrible significance...
Layer by layer, the facade of normalcy peeled away to reveal...
polish: Początkowo nie zauważyłem nic niepokojącego, ale stopniowo ogarnęło mnie poczucie nieprawości...
Pełna groza sytuacji dotarła do mnie powoli, straszliwymi etapami...
Fragment po fragmencie, przeraająca prawda układała się w moim umyśle...
To, co wydawało się jedynie dziwne, ujawniło teraz swoje prawdziwe i straszliwe znaczenie...
Warstwa po warstwie, fasada normalności opadała, by odsłonić...

delayedRevelation.description: Never reveal the horror immediately. Build through hints, suggestions, and partial glimpses.
stages: 1. Subtle wrongness - something is slightly off but undefined
2. Growing unease - the wrongness becomes more apparent
3. First glimpse - a partial, unclear view of the horror
4. Full realization - the complete nature is understood (but perhaps not described)
delayedRevelation.example: First describe only shadows and sounds. Then shapes. Then partial features. The full horror should be implied rather than stated.

anomalyOverMonster.description: Build horror from small wrong details, not from showing the creature. Players assemble the dread from fragments themselves.
techniques: Point at things that do not fit: too little blood, an angel statue's finger turned upside-down, a crypt suspiciously too clean, human bite-marks on a victim
Let players draw the terrifying conclusion - never state it outright
Prefer dread over horror: end the scene on the threshold, before the confrontation (the unexplored tunnel, the fleeing figure, the door left ajar)
anomalyOverMonster.example: Do not show the ghoul. Show the grave dug from the inside, the pickaxe left behind, the smell of wet fur - and stop.

horrorByEffect.description: Show the horror through its effect on observers rather than direct description.
techniques: Describe physical reactions: trembling, pallor, cold sweat, racing heart
Show mental effects: inability to focus, intrusive thoughts, nightmares
Use comparisons: "Never in my years of medical practice had I seen such..."
Reference lasting damage: "My hair turned white that night"

english: My knees nearly gave way beneath me...
A cold perspiration broke out upon my brow...
I felt the blood drain from my face...
My heart seemed to stop in my chest...
A terrible trembling seized my entire frame...
polish: Nogi niemal się pode mną ugięły...
Zimny pot wystąpił mi na czoło...
Poczułem, jak krew odpływa mi z twarzy...
Serce zdawało się zatrzymać w mojej piersi...
Straszliwe drżenie opanowało całe moje ciało...

sensoryHierarchy.description: Lovecraft typically engages senses in a specific order to build dread.
order: 1. SMELL - First hint of wrongness (decay, must, chemical, otherworldly odor)
2. SOUND - Strange noises that cannot be fully identified
3. TOUCH - Temperature changes, texture sensations, physical pressure
4. TASTE - Metallic tang, ozone, copper on the tongue: a reliable marker that something otherworldly is near, often before it is seen
5. SIGHT - The final, most impactful revelation (often partial or unclear)

cosmicPerspective.description: Emphasize the vastness of time, space, and the insignificance of humanity.
techniques: Reference geological or cosmic time scales
Contrast human civilization with elder races
Suggest that human concepts of good/evil are meaningless
Imply that attention from cosmic entities is worse than their indifference

english: In the face of such cosmic antiquity, all human history seemed but an eyeblink...
We are but ephemeral motes in an uncaring universe...
What we call evil is merely the indifference of forces beyond our comprehension...
Mankind clings to its comforting illusions of significance...
polish: W obliczu takiej kosmicznej starożytności, cała ludzka historia zdała się jedynie mgnieniem oka...
Jesteśmy jedynie efemerycznymi pyłkami w obojętnym wszechświecie...
To, co nazywamy złem, jest jedynie obojętnością sił poza naszym pojmowaniem...
Ludzkość kurczowo trzyma się pocieszających iluzji o własnym znaczeniu...

elements: Crumbling Georgian or Colonial architecture
Empty, sagging buildings with boarded windows
Overgrown streets and untended gardens
A pervasive smell of salt, decay, or mustiness
Silent, furtive inhabitants who avoid strangers
Ancient church or meeting house in disrepair
A harbor or waterfront past its prosperous days
decayedTown.atmosphere: Emphasize silence broken by wind, creaking signs, distant water. Colors are muted grays, greens, and browns. The town seems to exist in perpetual twilight or overcast.

elements: Ancient, twisted trees with gnarled limbs
Unnaturally dense undergrowth
Strange clearings with disturbed earth
Standing stones or ruined foundations
Unnatural silence - no birds, no insects
Paths that seem to shift or disappear
Areas where compass needles spin wildly
hauntedWoods.atmosphere: Canopy blocks most light. Air is heavy, moist, oppressive. Sound seems muffled. There is always a sense of being watched.

elements: Worn stone steps descending into darkness
Walls of cyclopean masonry or natural rock
Ancient inscriptions in unknown languages
Chambers of non-Euclidean geometry
Phosphorescent fungi or strange luminescence
Underground streams or pools of dark water
Altars or ritual spaces with disturbing relics
undergroundCrypt.atmosphere: Complete darkness requiring artificial light. Cold, damp air carrying strange odors. Echoes that seem to come from impossible directions.

elements: Sky of wrong color or containing wrong stars
Architecture following alien geometric principles
Vegetation utterly unlike Earth species
Light that seems to come from no source
Surfaces of impossible texture or material
Sounds that no earthly creature could produce
alienLandscape.atmosphere: All senses report wrongness. Normal physics may not apply. Time may flow differently.

physicalTraits: Wide, staring eyes with unusual quality
Coarse, thick skin with strange texture
behavioralTraits: Speaking in dialect or broken speech
Unnatural affinity for sea or specific locations

physicalTraits: Dimensions that seem wrong or impossible
Features that mix organic and geometric
Signs of prolonged exposure to the unnatural
behavioralTraits: Willingness to sacrifice others or self

actions: Offerings of blood or other substances
Use of drugs or altered states
sounds: Beating of drums in irregular rhythms
Words in languages that hurt to hear

necronomicon.author: Abdul Alhazred, the Mad Arab
necronomicon.content: Contains rites to summon the Great Old Ones, history of the elder races, astronomical-mystical formulae
necronomicon.danger: Reading it leads to madness; possessing it attracts unwanted attention

bookOfEibon.author: Eibon of Hyperborea
bookOfEibon.content: Sorcery of Tsathoggua, formulae of the left-hand path
bookOfEibon.danger: Unknown to most, considered a myth

pnakoticManuscripts.author: Unknown, possibly Great Race of Yith
pnakoticManuscripts.origin: Transcribed from mental projections across time
pnakoticManuscripts.content: History of Earth before humanity, secrets of time travel

atmosphere: ☐ Open scenes with environmental/sensory details before action
☐ Use weather and lighting to establish mood (fog, storms, twilight)
☐ Include at least one unusual or unsettling sensory detail per scene
☐ Reference the passage of time in ways that create unease
☐ Describe silence and absence as actively oppressive
horror: ☐ Build horror gradually - never reveal everything at once
☐ Describe effects on witnesses before describing the cause
☐ Leave some things deliberately undefined or impossible to describe
☐ Use physical symptoms of fear (cold sweat, trembling, pallor)
☐ Suggest that what is seen is only a fraction of what exists
language: ☐ Use archaic or elevated vocabulary sparingly but consistently
☐ Employ complex sentence structures for important revelations
☐ Include sensory verbs specific to each sense type
☐ Use negation to describe ("not quite", "almost but not", "like X but wrong")
☐ Reference scholarly or antiquarian knowledge when appropriate
cosmic: ☐ Occasionally remind players of cosmic scale and human insignificance
☐ Imply that current events connect to vast, incomprehensible patterns
☐ Present danger as indifference rather than malice
☐ Question the reliability of perception and sanity
